title: From chicken feathers to antimicrobial peptides for smart, self-disinfecting nanocoating
author: HES-SO
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: • Various hydrolysis methods are evaluated on poultry waste and the best approaches for optimal antimicrobial activity are selected for larger scale production. • Several extracted AMP mixtures demonstrate antimicrobial activity and are further purified to isolate the most active peptide fractions. • Studies are ongoing to explore the anchoring of bioactive keratin-based peptides on the surface of mesoporous copper-silica nanoparticles.
steps_raw: • Various hydrolysis methods are evaluated on poultry waste and the best approaches for optimal antimicrobial activity are selected for larger scale production. • Several extracted AMP mixtures demonstrate antimicrobial activity and are further purified to isolate the most active peptide fractions. • Studies are ongoing to explore the anchoring of bioactive keratin-based peptides on the surface of mesoporous copper-silica nanoparticles.
